rows.requirement: Explicit comparison of precision with vs. without graph-traversal expansion
rows.covered_by: compare_traversal_precision() runs retrieve_documents at expanded_max_hops and at max_hops=0, scores both via eval.metrics.score_arm

rows.requirement: Correctly flags a precision decrease when expansion adds a low-relevance neighbor (fixture scenario, per test spec verbatim)
Does not always report a decrease (no false positive when expansion adds nothing / adds only relevant docs)
Reports any checkpoint (of potentially several) where expansion decreases precision
Ollama-only / offline-testable -- zero live API calls
rows.covered_by: _StubLLMClient (in-file, deterministic, no network) used for all entity extraction in every test
rows.test: all tests in test_traversal_precision_check.py (no OllamaClient import, no httpx import)

rows.requirement: Reuse existing recall/precision + graph-traversal infra, no duplication
rows.covered_by: traversal_precision.py imports score_arm/ArmScore/QueryScore from eval.metrics and EntityGraph/retrieve_documents/DEFAULT_MAX_HOPS from eval.baselines.graphrag_lite; zero reimplementation
rows.test: manual code review (import statements) + no regression in eval/test_metrics_recall_precision.py or eval/test_graphrag_baseline.py
